title: senecio coquimbensis
intro: Senecio coquimbensis is a plant species in the daisy family, native to the Andes mountain range, and is also known as Chilca or Chilean groundsel. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Senecio coquimbensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and even exhibit antiviral properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
